extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 2.5km to go on Col de Turini, dropped VINGEGAARD and GULDØ in final 500m, won by clear margin after group hesitation.
tactical_patterns: UAE isolated POGAČAR early; FOSS as last Visma domestique dropped 6km out reveals team depth disparity on long climbs.
Breakaway given only 3-minute max gap on hardest stage suggests teams prioritized GC attacks over conserving energy for finale.
VINGEGAARD briefly closed POGAČAR's first acceleration then led chase himself, tactical error burning matches before decisive 2.5km move.
Top-10 looking around after VINGEGAARD rejoined allowed YATES/POULSEN to limit losses to 12s, hesitation cost POGAČAR bigger time gaps.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: won 6 of 12 racing days entering stage, launched winning move 2.5km out after failed earlier acceleration showed patience progression
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: closed initial POGAČAR attack and limited losses to manageable seconds, vastly superior to Stage 4 Faron collapse
rider_signals.FOSS Tobias: negative: only remaining Visma domestique dropped 6km from finish on critical GC stage exposes team selection weakness
rider_signals.JORGENSEN Mattias: negative: dropped before final 4km when lead group whittled to three, clear tier below top GC contenders
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: positive: finished 7th at 35s despite INEOS working early for breakaway control, strong form as domestique-turned-GC threat
rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: negative: crashed with 15km to go losing contact with leaders, poor positioning cost potential top-5 finish
